title: Linet Eleganza Hospital Bed - Actuator or Positioning Motor Failure
description: Identifies causes of slow, unresponsive, or non-functioning bed positioning motors (hi-lo, backrest, knee gatch) including power, connections, and mechanical obstructions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred or fully supported. Do not operate or troubleshoot actuators while the patient is on the bed.

steps.title: Check Power Supply
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is plugged into a live outlet. Check battery backup charge if equipped. Expected Outcome: Bed should respond to controls if power is adequate.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cords and Connectors
steps.instructions: Examine for loose, damaged, or disconnected cables to actuators and control panels. Expected Outcome: Restoring proper connections may resume normal function.

steps.title: Test Control Panel Commands
steps.instructions: Operate each motor function individually (hi-lo, backrest, knee gatch). Listen for actuator noise or observe movement. Expected Outcome: If one actuator fails while others work, the issue is likely isolated.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect bed frame, joints, and mattress positioning for interference. Expected Outcome: Freeing any obstruction may restore movement.

steps.title: Cycle Actuators Without Load
steps.instructions: With no patient, run each motor to full range to observe operation. Expected Outcome: Detect slow, uneven, or non-functioning actuators, confirming mechanical or motor issues.

CCR.Complaint: Bed backrest and hi-lo motors unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Actuators failed to move after verifying power and connections.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual sounds, heat, or smell
Final bed status: Out of Service

title: Linet Eleganza Hospital Bed - Side Rail Control Not Responding
description: Identifies causes of unresponsive side rail controls, including power, lockout settings, cable connections, and panel faults before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely positioned or transferred. Do not attempt control tests with the patient reliant on the side rail.

steps.title: Check Bed Lockouts
steps.instructions: Verify that side rail lockout features are not engaged. If active, controls will not respond. Deactivating lockouts should restore functionality.

steps.title: Inspect Power Supply
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is plugged in, battery is charged, and the main power switch is on. Unpowered controls cannot operate.

steps.title: Examine Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check all cables connecting side rails to the main control box for secure connections, damage, or loose pins. Replace or reseat as needed.

steps.title: Test Both Side Rails
steps.instructions: Operate controls on both sides to determine if the issue is isolated to one rail or affects both, indicating panel versus wiring issues.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights and Sounds
steps.instructions: Look for fault LEDs or error beeps on the side rail or main control unit, which may indicate a detected malfunction.

steps.title: Swap Side Rail Panels (If Available)
steps.instructions: If another identical panel is available, swapping can isolate whether the panel itself is defective versus wiring or control electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Nurse reports left side rail control does not respond.
CCR.Cause: Side rail panel cable was loose and lockout feature engaged.
CCR.Resolution: Secured cable and disengaged lockout; controls function restored.

title: Linet Eleganza Hospital Bed - Bed Exit Alarm False Alarms
description: Identifies causes of false bed exit alarms and guides troubleshooting for sensors, mattress placement, and alarm settings.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely supported or transferred to another bed before testing alarms. Patient safety takes priority over troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Mattress and Sensor Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is properly seated and aligned. Verify that bed exit sensors or pressure mats are correctly positioned and free from folds or obstructions. Misalignment often triggers false alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Side Rails and Accessories
steps.instructions: Confirm side rails are fully up or down as intended, and remove or adjust any accessories (blankets, cushions, overlays) that may activate sensors.

steps.title: Verify Sensor Connections
steps.instructions: Check all cables from the bed exit sensors to the control panel. Ensure connectors are seated firmly and undamaged. Loose or damaged connections can create intermittent alarms.

steps.title: Check Control Panel Settings
steps.instructions: Review alarm sensitivity settings on the bed control panel. Ensure they are appropriate for the patient’s weight and mobility. Reset to default if uncertain.

steps.title: Test Bed on a Known Good Outlet
steps.instructions: Move the bed to a different power outlet if possible. Electrical interference or fluctuations can sometimes cause false alarms.

steps.title: Observe Alarm Behavior During Testing
steps.instructions: Without a patient, gently simulate movement on the mattress to confirm the alarm triggers appropriately. Ensure it does not activate unexpectedly.

CCR.Complaint: Bed exit alarm activates without patient movement.
CCR.Cause: Sensor misaligned under mattress and sensitivity set too high.
CCR.Resolution: Realigned sensor, adjusted sensitivity, tested alarm function.

title: Linet Eleganza Hospital Bed - Scale Errors or Calibration Drift
description: Addresses inaccurate or drifting weight readings caused by mattress placement, patient movement, accessories, or environmental factors before assuming internal scale failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Do not attempt scale adjustments with a patient on the bed.

steps.title: Check Bed Leveling and Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is on a flat, stable surface. Uneven flooring can cause weight inaccuracies. Expected outcome: readings should stabilize after proper leveling.

steps.title: Verify Mattress Position and Accessories
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is properly seated and any overlays or pads are evenly distributed. Misalignment can affect scale accuracy.

steps.title: Inspect Power Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is plugged into a functioning outlet and that the cord is undamaged. Expected outcome: scale powers on without error.

steps.title: Zero or Tare the Scale
steps.instructions: Follow Linet’s external zero/tare procedure (without patient). Expected outcome: scale returns to 0 kg or lbs.

steps.title: Check for Interference or Excess Load
steps.instructions: Remove any additional equipment or weight on the bed (IV pumps, sheets, items on mattress). Expected outcome: scale reading reflects only mattress weight.

steps.title: Test with Known Weight
steps.instructions: Place a certified test weight or object of known mass on the bed. Expected outcome: scale reading matches test weight within tolerance.

CCR.Complaint: Bed scale shows inconsistent or drifting weight readings.
CCR.Cause: Mattress misalignment and residual load interference found during troubleshooting.
CCR.Resolution: Mattress repositioned, excess items removed, scale zeroed, and reading verified with test weight.

title: Linet Eleganza Hospital Bed - Power Supply or Battery Failure
description: Addresses bed power loss or battery failure caused by external power, cords, fuses, or battery connections before assuming internal electronics issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Power troubleshooting should never occur with a patient relying on the bed.

steps.title: Verify Wall Outlet and Power Source
steps.instructions:
Action: Plug a known-working device (lamp, monitor) into the same outlet.
Expected Outcome: Outlet provides consistent power.
Why: Eliminates external power supply as the source of failure.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions:
Action: Check for visible damage, kinks, or loose connections. Ensure the cord is fully seated in both the bed and outlet.
Expected Outcome: Secure, undamaged connections.
Why: Faulty cords or loose plugs are common causes of power failure.

steps.title: Check Bed Power Switch and Fuses
steps.instructions:
Action: Ensure the bed’s main power switch is on. Inspect accessible fuses for continuity if applicable.
Expected Outcome: Switch in ON position; fuses intact.
Why: Some beds have fuses or circuit breakers that prevent operation if blown.

steps.title: Assess Battery Condition
steps.instructions:
Action: Inspect the battery for corrosion, swelling, or loose terminals. Confirm battery is seated correctly.
Expected Outcome: Battery connections are secure; no visible damage.
Why: Weak or disconnected batteries often mimic total power loss.

steps.title: Test Bed with Alternate Power Source
steps.instructions:
Action: If possible, connect the bed to a known-working outlet or battery.
Expected Outcome: Bed powers on normally.
Why: Confirms whether the issue is internal or external.

steps.title: Observe Bed Indicators
steps.instructions:
Action: Look for LED indicators or display messages when attempting to power on.
Expected Outcome: Normal status lights indicate power is restored; absence suggests internal electronics issue.

CCR.Complaint: Bed will not power on, bedside staff reports no response from controls.
CCR.Cause: External power verified; battery and cord inspection revealed no faults; internal electronics suspected.
CCR.Resolution: Bed labeled Out of Service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Final bed status: Out of Service

title: Linet Multicare Hospital Bed - Actuator or Positioning Motor Failure
description: Identifies causes of slow, unresponsive, or non-functioning positioning motors (hi-lo, backrest, knee gatch) including power, connections, and mechanical obstructions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Never test actuators with a patient reliant on the bed.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is plugged into a working outlet. Inspect the power cord for damage or loose connections. Confirm battery (if equipped) is charged. Expected Outcome: Bed powers on or shows battery status.

steps.title: Inspect Main Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions: Locate and check fuses on the bed’s control box. Reset any tripped breakers. Expected Outcome: Bed functions if fuse/trip was the issue.

steps.title: Test Control Handset and Side Rails
steps.instructions: Operate each motor function via the handset and any side rail controls. Listen for motor sounds or movement. Expected Outcome: Motors respond to commands; note which functions fail.

steps.title: Check Motor Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect visible actuator cables for secure connections and damage. Re-seat connectors if loose. Expected Outcome: Restores function if loose connections were the cause.

steps.title: Inspect Mechanical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Look for items obstructing bed movement or stuck gatches. Ensure bed sections move freely without resistance. Expected Outcome: Motors operate smoothly if obstruction removed.

steps.title: Observe for Unusual Noise or Heat
steps.instructions: Listen for grinding or buzzing motors. Feel for excess heat at motor housings. Expected Outcome: Normal sound indicates motor healthy; unusual signs indicate internal issue.

CCR.Complaint: Bed backrest and knee gatch not responding to controls.
CCR.Cause: Loose actuator cable and partially obstructed gatch.
CCR.Resolution: Re-seated cable and cleared obstruction; functions restored.

helpfulDetails: Handset and side rail controls tested
Any unusual heat or resistance noted
Final device status: operational or OOS

title: Linet Multicare Hospital Bed - Side Rail Control Not Responding
description: Identifies causes of unresponsive side rail controls, including power, lockout functions, cable connections, or panel faults before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred or fully supported. Never attempt side rail control troubleshooting with the patient relying on the bed.

steps.title: Verify Bed Power
steps.instructions:
Check that the bed is connected to a live outlet.
Confirm that any battery backup is charged.
Expected Outcome: Bed electronics should have power; indicator lights are on.

steps.title: Inspect Side Rail Lockout Settings
steps.instructions:
Confirm the side rail is not in a mechanical or electronic lockout mode.
Expected Outcome: Control should be active if lockout is disabled.

steps.title: Check Control Panel and Cables
steps.instructions:
Inspect the side rail control panel for visible damage, dirt, or stuck buttons.
Verify connections between the panel and the bed’s control box.
Expected Outcome: Secure cables and a clean panel may restore function.

steps.title: Test Alternate Control
steps.instructions:
Use bed-mounted or remote nurse control if available.
Expected Outcome: If alternate controls work, the issue is likely the side rail panel itself.

steps.title: Observe for Indicator Feedback
steps.instructions:
Look for lights or error messages on the side rail panel or bed display.
Expected Outcome: Indicators may help identify panel-specific faults.

CCR.Complaint: Side rail control unresponsive when attempting to raise or lower the rail.
CCR.Cause: Control panel cable loose; lockout mode engaged.
CCR.Resolution: Secured cable connection, disabled lockout; verified side rail movement restored.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: functional or sent for repair

title: Linet Multicare Hospital Bed - Bed Exit Alarm False Alarms
description: Identifies causes of false bed exit alarms, including sensor misalignment, patient movement, mattress positioning, and electrical interference.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely supported or transferred to another bed before testing alarms. Never compromise patient safety during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Bed Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is level and fully flat. Uneven surfaces can trigger sensors falsely. Expected outcome: bed stability reduces false alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress Placement
steps.instructions: Verify the mattress is correctly positioned without folds, gaps, or non-standard pads. Misalignment may cause pressure or tilt sensors to activate.

steps.title: Examine Side Rails and Locks
steps.instructions: Confirm side rails are fully locked and functioning. Partial engagement or loose rails can simulate patient exit.

steps.title: Review Alarm Settings and Delay Timers
steps.instructions: Check the bed exit alarm sensitivity and delay settings. Excessively high sensitivity or zero delay can cause nuisance alarms. Adjust as appropriate per facility protocol.

steps.title: Check Sensor Cables and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect all sensor wiring for loose, pinched, or disconnected cables. A secure connection should restore normal alarm function.

steps.title: Test with a Simulated Weight or Sensor Activation
steps.instructions: Activate the bed exit sensor manually or with a test weight to confirm alarm function. Expected outcome: alarm triggers only when appropriate.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported frequent false bed exit alarms on the Multicare bed.
CCR.Cause: Sensor misalignment and mattress placement issues identified.
CCR.Resolution: Mattress repositioned, sensors checked, and alarm settings adjusted. Bed returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Final bed status: fully functional, no false alarms

title: Linet Multicare Hospital Bed - Scale Errors or Calibration Drift
description: Addresses inaccurate or drifting weight readings due to mattress placement, patient movement, accessories, or environmental factors before assuming internal scale failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Never troubleshoot scale functions with a patient on the bed.

steps.title: Check Mattress Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is correctly seated and aligned on the bed frame. Misalignment can trigger inaccurate readings. Expect proper mattress fit to stabilize weight display.

steps.title: Remove Accessories or Bed Items
steps.instructions: Detach sheets, pads, or monitoring equipment that may press unevenly on sensors. Expect readings to normalize without extra pressure on the scale.

steps.title: Inspect Power and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is connected to a reliable power source or fully charged battery. Inspect cables to the scale interface for secure connections. Loose or intermittent power can cause drift.

steps.title: Observe Bed on Level Surface
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is on a flat, stable floor. Uneven floors can affect load cells. Expect consistent readings when the bed is level.

steps.title: Reset Scale Function
steps.instructions: If the bed provides a scale reset or zero function, perform this with the mattress removed or empty. Expect zeroed reading as confirmation of proper reset.

steps.title: Check Indicator Lights or Alarms
steps.instructions: Verify any scale-related error lights or alarms on the bed control panel. Persistent warnings may indicate a deeper issue.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported inconsistent weight readings on the Multicare bed.
CCR.Cause: Mattress misalignment and residual bedding caused scale drift.
CCR.Resolution: Realigned mattress, removed excess bedding, and reset scale function; readings stabilized.

title: Linet Multicare Hospital Bed - Power Supply or Battery Failure
description: Addresses loss of bed power or battery backup failure caused by outlet issues, cords, fuses, or battery connections before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Never troubleshoot power issues with a patient on the bed.

steps.title: Check External Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify the wall outlet is live using a known working device or outlet tester. Expected outcome: device should receive power when plugged in.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Examine the bed’s power cord for damage, kinks, or loose connections. Ensure the plug is fully seated. Expected outcome: no visible damage, secure connections.

steps.title: Check Bed Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions: Locate any external fuses or breakers on the bed and inspect for tripped circuits or blown fuses. Replace or reset if needed. Expected outcome: fuse intact or replaced, bed powered on.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Status
steps.instructions: If the bed has a removable or internal battery, ensure it is properly seated and charged. Expected outcome: battery connected, charging indicator active.

steps.title: Test Alternate Power Source
steps.instructions: If possible, plug the bed into a different, known working outlet. Expected outcome: bed powers on, indicating original outlet may be faulty.

steps.title: Observe Bed Indicators
steps.instructions: Power lights, alarms, or status LEDs should illuminate. Lack of indicators may suggest battery or internal power module failure.

CCR.Complaint: Bed would not power on; patient unable to use controls.
CCR.Cause: Bed was plugged into a non-functional outlet; battery seated correctly.
CCR.Resolution: Outlet tested and verified; cord checked; bed powered on successfully.
